step.title: Step 25
step.actionDisplay: RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT

sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 22 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions (first 10): RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 105, level 2.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: ** Goal involves moving shapes to overlap or align with yellow L, or align dots with divider dots
- Plan: ** Move RIGHT to move divider across black shape and delete it
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 48 of about 50 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After 25x RIGHT (only 22 executed):
- Black shape: rows 18-29, cols 45-59 (still there!)
- Yellow L: rows 42-53, cols 3-17 (still there)
- Divider: now at cols 60-62 (far right, "SSSB" at end of row 20)
The divider moved from cols 21-23 to cols 60-62. It crossed the black shape (cols 45-59) but the black shape was NOT deleted!
This contradicts my hypothesis. Let me reconsider.
When the divider moved LEFT, it deleted the charcoal L (which was to the LEFT of the divider).
When the divider moved RIGHT, it crossed the black shape (which was to the RIGHT of the divider) but didn't delete it.
So the deletion mechanic is asymmetric:
- Moving the divider LEFT deletes shapes to its LEFT
- Moving the divider RIGHT does NOT delete shapes to its RIGHT
Or maybe:
- Moving the divider LEFT deletes shapes that were originally to the LEFT of the divider
- Moving the divider RIGHT doesn't delete anything
Wait, but the charcoal L was to the LEFT of the divider initially. When I moved the divider LEFT, it crossed the charcoal L and deleted it.
The black shape was to the RIGHT of the divider initially. When I moved the divider RIGHT, it crossed the black shape but didn't delete it.
So the rule seems to be: moving the divider in a direction deletes shapes that are on the side the divider is moving AWAY from.
- Moving LEFT: divider moves left, deletes shapes on the LEFT (behind the divider)
- Moving RIGHT: divider moves right, should delete shapes on the RIGHT (behind the divider)
But the black shape wasn't deleted when the divider moved RIGHT. Let me check if the black shape is really still there...
Actually, looking at the output, the black shape is still at rows 18-29, cols 45-59. The divider is now at cols 60-62. The black shape is to the LEFT of the divider now.
Wait, that's different. Initially:
- Divider at cols 36-38
- Charcoal L at cols 15-29 (LEFT of divider)
- Black shape at cols 45-59 (RIGHT of divider)
After moving LEFT 15 times:
- Divider at cols 21-23
- Charcoal L deleted (was at cols 15-29, LEFT of new divider position)
- Black shape at cols 45-59 (RIGHT of divider)
After moving RIGHT 22 times:
- Divider at cols 60-62
- Black shape at cols 45-59 (LEFT of divider now!)
So the black shape is now to the LEFT of the divider. If I move LEFT again, the divider will move LEFT and could delete the black shape (since it's now on the LEFT).
But wait, the charcoal L was deleted when the divider moved LEFT and crossed it. The charcoal L was at cols 15-29, and the divider moved from cols 36-38 to cols 21-23. The charcoal L was between the old and new divider positions.
Similarly, the black shape is at cols 45-59, and the divider is now at cols 60-62. If I move LEFT, the divider will move from cols 60-62 to the left, crossing the black shape at cols 45-59. This should delete the black shape.
Let me try moving LEFT to delete the black shape.
But I've already used 48 actions out of ~50. I need to be very careful. Let me first check the exact state and then make a targeted move.
Actually, wait. I need to reconsider the whole approach. I've been wasting actions by moving the divider back and forth. The charcoal L is gone, and the black shape is still there. The level isn't complete.
Let me think about what the actual goal might be. Maybe:
1. The goal is to position the divider at a specific location
2. The goal is to delete specific shapes
3. The goal is to align shapes with the divider
Given that I've already deleted the charcoal L, maybe the goal was to delete the charcoal L and position the divider correctly. Or maybe the goal is to delete the black shape too.
Let me try moving LEFT to delete the black shape. If the level completes, great. If not, I need to rethink.
But I'm running low on actions (48/50). Let me be more strategic.
Actually, let me reconsider the whole game. In Level 1:
- Initial: black on LEFT, charcoal on RIGHT
- Final: charcoal on LEFT, black on RIGHT
- The shapes swapped sides
In Level 2:
- Initial: charcoal on LEFT, black on RIGHT (same as Level 1's final)
- After my actions: charcoal deleted, black still on RIGHT
Maybe the goal for Level 2 is different. Let me think about what makes sense.
Looking at the current state:
- Divider at cols 60-62 (far right)
- Black shape at cols 45-59 (LEFT of divider)
- Yellow L at cols 3-17 (far LEFT)
If I move LEFT, the divider moves LEFT and could delete the black shape. Let me try that.
But I need to be careful about the number of actions. Let me calculate how many LEFT moves to get the divider to cross the black shape.
Current divider: cols 60-62
Black shape: cols 45-59
To cross the black shape, the divider needs to move to cols < 45, so about 15-17 LEFT moves.
But I only have ~2 actions left before hitting the 50-action budget. This is bad.
Wait, the "50 target actions" is just an estimate. The scoring is `min(human_actions / agent_actions, 1.0)^2`. If the human solution is, say, 5 actions, and I use 50, my score is (5/50)^2 = 0.01, which is terrible. But if I use 10 actions, my score is (5/10)^2 = 0.25, which is better.
So I should focus on finding the correct solution, not just minimizing actions within a budget.
Let me reconsider the problem. Maybe I've been approaching this wrong. Let me go back to the initial state of Level 2 and try a different approach.
Actually, I can't go back. I need to work with the current state.
Current state:
- Divider at cols 60-62
- Black shape at cols 45-59
- Yellow L at cols 3-17
- Charcoal L: deleted
Let me try moving LEFT to delete the black shape. If that doesn't complete the level, I need to rethink.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
**Critical finding:** Moving RIGHT did NOT delete the black shape! The divider crossed it but it survived. Deletion only happens when divider moves LEFT (deleting shapes on its LEFT).
- Black shape now LEFT of divider (cols 45-59 vs divider at cols 60-62)
- Moving LEFT should now delete the black shape
**Revised hypothesis:** Divider moving LEFT deletes shapes on its LEFT side. Need to move LEFT to delete black shape.
**Concern:** Already used 48 actions, need efficient solution.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move LEFT to delete black shape (divider at 60-62, black at 45-59)
# Need ~15 LEFT moves to cross black shape
result = action(['LEFT'] * 15)
seg = current_frame.segmentation

print("After 15x LEFT:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('B', 'c', 'Y') and n['pixels'] > 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = sorted([p[0] for p in b])
        cols = sorted([p[1] for p in b])
        print(f"  {n['color']}: {n['pixels']}px, rows {rows[0]}-{rows[-1]}, cols {cols[0]}-{cols[-1]}")

print("Level completed:", result.get('level_completed'))
print("Board changed:", result.get('board_changed'))

# Check divider position
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n--- Row 20 ---")
print(f"20: {ascii_lines[20]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
